title: ophioglossum vulgatum var. polyphyllum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum var. polyphyllum, also known as the resurrection fern or adder's tongue fern, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Ophioglossum, and is characterized by its unique ability to survive in dry conditions by drying out and then reviving when water becomes available. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum var. polyphyllum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and pain, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its leaves and rhizomes, and are often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures.
